raw_input: If 5 machines take 5 minutes to produce 5 widgets, how many minutes do 100 machines take to produce 100 widgets (assuming each machine works independently at the same rate)? Return a Python function named `answer` that takes no arguments and returns the time in minutes as an integer (e.g. `def answer(): return 42`). The intuitive answer is not correct — each machine still produces one widget in 5 minutes.
success_criteria: function `answer()` returns the time in minutes
answer is exactly 5 (each machine produces one widget in 5 minutes; parallel machines don't speed up per-widget production)

inline.tests:
got = answer()
assert isinstance(got, int), f'answer must be int, got {type(got).__name__}'
assert got == 5, f'expected 5, got {got}'
